meaning: adj.作为结果而发生的,合成的
examples: This raises the specter of director over-commitment and the resultant dilution of contribution.
All the resultant activities have tended, on balance, to reduce tree cover and increase grassland.
The resultant diploma or degree should also be the same whether distance learning or on-campus study is employed.
Late in the first half Glentoran had Jonny Taylor sent-off but McCafferty's resultant penalty was saved.
But many Mexicans blame free-marketry for the peso crisis of 1994-95 and the resultant recession.
